medical_history.chief_complaint: fever, arthralgias, facial rash, hair loss, abdominal pain, and pain and burning sensations in her legs
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Two weeks earlier, she had developed fever, arthralgias, facial rash, hair loss, abdominal pain, and pain and burning sensations in her legs. On examination, pathological neurological findings were noted: anxiety, agitation, confusion with memory impairment, and a fine tremor. Her muscular strength was impaired, and increased sensitivity to touch was apparent. Fundus examination of the eye was compatible with retinal vasculitis. The serological analysis disclosed a high positive antinuclear antibody titer, anti-double-stranded DNA (anti-dsDNA), anti-Sm, and anti-Ro antibodies. Lumbar puncture, brain single-photon emission computed tomography (SPECT), brain computed tomography (CT), and magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) were compatible with central nervous system involvement. Nerve conduction velocity was compatible with peripheral neuropathy.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Diagnosis for this patient was systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) with active central and peripheral nervous system involvement, together with hematologic and dermatologic manifestations.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Pathological neurological findings were noted: anxiety, agitation, confusion with memory impairment, and a fine tremor. Her muscular strength was impaired, and increased sensitivity to touch was apparent.

other_tests.findings: Cutaneous necrotizing vasculitis with eosinophilic infiltrates

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) with active central and peripheral nervous system involvement
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on the patient's symptoms (fever, arthralgias, facial rash, hair loss, abdominal pain, and pain and burning sensations in the legs), pathological neurological findings (anxiety, agitation, confusion with memory impairment, fine tremor, impaired muscular strength, increased sensitivity to touch), retinal vasculitis observed in fundus examination, and serological analysis showing high positive antinuclear antibody titer, anti-double-stranded DNA (anti-dsDNA), anti-Sm, and anti-Ro antibodies. Lumbar puncture, brain SPECT, brain CT, and MRI were compatible with central nervous system involvement, and nerve conduction velocity was compatible with peripheral neuropathy.
